- bugs found: workbench read fetches did not pass the active org header, so real-data reads could fall back to the wrong org and show false empty/error states. `/set` also treated a successful no-SetPlan calendar queue as an acquisition failure and rendered `実データ未取得`/`実データを取得できませんでした` even when the API read itself succeeded with no calendar-backed work.
- security risks found: no auth, authorization, permission, RLS policy, schema, migration, DB write, external send, secret, audit log, or mutation behavior changed. Org scoping was tightened for client read fetches by sending the current `x-org-id`.
- performance issues found: no new polling, write path, DB read fan-out, or backend workload was introduced. The client reuses the active org scope and distinguishes empty vs failed calendar reads without extra normal-path network calls.
- validation commands: `pnpm vitest run src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.test.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-mutations.test.tsx src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.test.ts src/app/api/dispense-workbench/patients/route.test.ts src/lib/dispensing/dispense-workbench-shared.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm eslint src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.tsx src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-mutations.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.test.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --check src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.tsx src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-mutations.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.test.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.tsx src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-mutations.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.test.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.test.ts`; read-only local Prisma/API checks; authenticated Playwright desktop/mobile checks on `http://localhost:3012/dispense` and `/set`.

- bugs found: visit-record detail GET returned SOAP/receipt/reason/attachment/baseline-context PHI without sensitive no-store headers; unexpected read failures could escape as framework errors instead of a fixed no-store API envelope; protected GET matrix did not assert 401/403 no-store for the detail read; catalog/meta tests did not explicitly pin the base visit-record list/detail entries; GET reads happened outside `withOrgContext` even though `VisitRecord` is RLS/FORCE RLS protected; and the full record spread could return `visit_geo_log` while only deleting `patient_state_snapshot`.
- security risks found: reduced visit-record PHI/cache, tenant-isolation, geolocation, snapshot, and raw-error exposure risk by wrapping handled GET responses and unexpected failures with `Cache-Control: private, no-store, max-age=0` and `Pragma: no-cache`, returning a fixed `INTERNAL_ERROR` 500 envelope without raw error text, running all GET reads through `withOrgContext(ctx.orgId, ..., { requestContext: ctx })` and scoped `tx` delegates, and removing both `patient_state_snapshot` and `visit_geo_log` before the raw success response spread. Existing `canVisit`, `org_id`, assignment access, baseline context derivation, attachment normalization, raw top-level success shape except the two sensitive fields, PATCH behavior, schema, migrations, external sends, and destructive operations are unchanged.
- performance issues found: none materially changed. Valid detail reads keep the same logical query set but now execute under the RLS context; no new DB reads or N+1 paths were introduced.

- bugs found: full `GET /api/patients` list responses exposed nested `contacts[].phone/email/fax`, `cases[].care_team_links[].phone/email/fax`, `latest_case.care_team_links[].phone/email/fax`, and `scheduling_preference.preferred_contact_phone/name` even though known full-list callers only need identity/list status fields. The first in-progress sanitizer would also have broken `foundation_issue=missing_contact` and `foundation_issue=missing_care_team` because post-filters used sanitized contacts and care-team links.

### 20260626-0012 JST

- current task: continue Codex-only backend/API/privacy hardening by strict-validating explicit query values and no-store wrapping all `GET /api/prescription-intakes` responses.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox; `git status --short --untracked-files=all`; local Next.js Route Handlers guide from the prior route-hardening loop; `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts`; `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`; `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; prescription/search callers in `src/app/(dashboard)/prescriptions/prescriptions-workspace.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/search/search-content.tsx`, and `src/lib/search/categories.ts`; `src/lib/api/sensitive-response.ts`; `src/lib/auth/context.ts`; gbrain call-graph output from the pre-edit inspection; API-contract and privacy subagent reports.
- files changed: `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts`, `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `GET /api/prescription-intakes` silently normalized malformed explicit list/search filters: blank `q`, `status`, `source_type`, `care_tags`, and `include_total` were treated as omitted/false; padded values were trimmed or passed into enum checks inconsistently; overlong `q` was truncated to 100 characters; duplicate query keys used first-value semantics. This could widen a user-intended scoped prescription intake query or make malformed search input hit DB. The route also returned prescription/patient list/search data without sensitive no-store headers, and handler-only no-store wrapping would have missed auth/permission failures returned by `withAuthContext`.
- security risks found: reduced PHI/PII cache and overfetch risk by wrapping the exported GET route so 200, validation 400, auth 401, and permission 403 responses receive `Cache-Control: private, no-store, max-age=0` and `Pragma: no-cache`; malformed explicit query values now fail before assignment-scope and prescription-intake DB reads; validation errors use fixed field messages without echoing query values. Existing `canVisit` permission, assignment access scoping, valid omitted-filter list behavior, valid single-value status/source/care-tag filters, valid `include_total=1`, valid `include_total=0`, search minimal projection, POST create flow, schema, migrations, external send, and destructive operations are unchanged.
- performance issues found: malformed query requests now return before assignment/search where construction and before `prescriptionIntake.findMany`/`count`. Valid and omitted-filter requests keep the existing `limit + 1` cursor-page query behavior and optional count path.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-post-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; caller compatibility `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/(dashboard)/prescriptions/prescriptions-workspace.test.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/search/search-content.test.tsx' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --write src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --check src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` under token `E8717502-4C9D-4FC9-9665-75BA7C880E3F`.

### 20260625-2200 JST

- current task: follow up on the patient-share-cases privacy review by minimizing `patient_share_cases_viewed` audit payloads for list views.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox; `git status --short --untracked-files=all`; `src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts`; `src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts`; `src/lib/audit-log.ts`; `src/app/api/audit-logs/export/route.ts`; the prior patient-share-cases privacy reviewer finding; local validation output for the patient-share-cases and protected-route suites.

- bugs found: `GET /api/patient-share-cases` wrote page-level share-case, base-patient, base-site, and partner-pharmacy id arrays into `patient_share_cases_viewed` audit `changes`, and used the first returned share case as the aggregate list-view `targetId`. Unfiltered list views therefore persisted row-level identifiers unrelated to a single target and exposed those identifiers through audit-log consumers.
- security risks found: reduced privacy/audit-minimization risk by replacing row id arrays with count-only fields and using the stable aggregate `targetId: patient_share_cases` for list-view audits. Patient-specific filtered views still set `patientId` from the validated `base_patient_id`; query validation, `canManagePatientSharing`, org scoping, no-store headers, response redaction, POST create behavior, schema, migrations, frontend, external send, and destructive operations are unchanged.
- performance issues found: no DB query shape changed. The route now reuses the already-fetched visible page rows to compute small `Set` counts, and the persisted audit JSON is smaller.

### 20260625-2156 JST

- bugs found: `GET /api/patient-share-cases` was guarded by `canVisit`, while neighboring pharmacy-cooperation list routes such as `GET /api/pharmacy-visit-requests` and `GET /api/partner-visit-records` are already `canManagePatientSharing`. This allowed visit-only roles such as `pharmacist_trainee` to enumerate org-wide patient-share-case metadata without patient-sharing lifecycle authority.
- security risks found: reduced least-privilege risk by requiring `canManagePatientSharing` for the patient-share-case list route and aligning the route catalog. The new route test proves a `pharmacist_trainee` receives `403 AUTH_FORBIDDEN` before `withOrgContext`, `patientShareCase.findMany`, or `patient_share_cases_viewed` audit writes. Existing `pharmacist`/`admin` access, query validation from the previous slice, no-store headers, response redaction, POST create permission, schema, migrations, frontend, external send, and destructive operations are unchanged.
- performance issues found: unauthorized visit-only roles now fail before DB and audit writes. Authorized list behavior remains unchanged.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --check src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-post-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `git diff --check -- src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` under token `DE804878-DE3B-4CC9-AF84-2AB47B378108`.

- files inspected: agmsg inbox; `git status --short --untracked-files=all`; local Next.js Route Handlers guide `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/15-route-handlers.md`; `src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.ts`; `src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts`; `src/phos/api/client.ts`; `src/phos/api/client.test.ts`; `src/phos/api/types.ts`; `src/phos/backend/input-validation.ts`; `src/phos/contracts/phos_contracts.ts`; `src/phos/infra/api-gateway-routes.ts`; gbrain call-graph outputs; security and API-contract subagent reports.
- files changed: `src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts`, `src/phos/api/client.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: the local PHOS proxy authenticated and catalog-checked requests, then copied all incoming query pairs to the upstream URL without aggregate length, param-count, per-key, per-value, or duplicate-key bounds.
- security risks found: reduced authenticated resource-boundary/availability risk by rejecting oversized PHOS proxy query strings before constructing/fetching the upstream URL. The guard now caps encoded query length at 8192, param pairs at 32, encoded key length at 64, encoded value length at 2048, and rejects duplicate query keys because the typed PHOS client and backend query readers use single-value params. New local proxy validation errors use canonical PHOS `ErrorResponse` shape with no raw query values in details, so same-origin clients preserve the error instead of normalizing it to `INTERNAL_ERROR`. Existing auth token lookup, route catalog allowlist, upstream base URL normalization, forwarded header allowlist, redirect blocking, timeout, normal query forwarding, and response header allowlist are preserved.
- performance issues found: oversized query requests now fail before upstream URL append/fetch and before pushing large request targets into the PHOS upstream/API Gateway path. Normal typed-client queries remain unchanged.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts' src/phos/api/client.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.ts' 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts' src/phos/api/client.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --check 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.ts' 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts' src/phos/api/client.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.ts' 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts' src/phos/api/client.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` under token `EE9DA61B-DEFD-4EF4-9136-5878F2BB2263`.
- validation results: focused PHOS proxy/client Vitest passed `2` files / `55` tests; focused ESLint passed; focused Prettier check passed; focused diff-check passed; `pnpm typecheck` passed and the local long-gate lock was released. Security reviewer rated the missing bound Medium and suggested 8192/32/64/2048; API-contract reviewer confirmed client compatibility and requested canonical local proxy errors plus duplicate-key rejection, both implemented.
- remaining work: stage only the PHOS proxy/client test files plus this Ralph state entry, commit the slice, send `DONE` to agmsg, close reviewer subagents, and continue backend/API hardening. Leave untracked `.agent-loop/plans/*` untouched.
- next action: drain agmsg, run final scoped Prettier/diff-check including this Ralph state entry, and commit the PHOS proxy query bounds slice.

- bugs found: `GET /api/inquiry-records?patient_id=` and `?cycle_id=` treated explicitly blank clinical filters as omitted filters, widening the request to the org/assignment-scoped inquiry list. `status=` had the same explicit-empty ambiguity. The route also returned clinical inquiry content without sensitive no-store headers, and offered no bounded path for callers that want a capped list.
- security risks found: reduced PHI/clinical-content overfetch risk by rejecting present-but-empty `patient_id`, `cycle_id`, and `status` before DB access; added `withSensitiveNoStore` to 200 and validation-error responses. No auth/authz permission semantics, RLS, DB schema, migration, billing, frontend, external send, or destructive behavior changed.
- performance issues found: explicit `limit` requests now apply `take: limit + 1`, trim the response to `limit`, and return additive `meta: { limit, has_more }`. After medical-safety review, no-limit calls intentionally keep the previous no-`take`, no-`meta` full-list behavior to avoid silently hiding older patient inquiry records from existing UI callers.

- files changed: `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.ts`, `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `dispatchWebhookEventForOrg` loaded every active webhook registration for an org, including registrations for unrelated events, then filtered by `registration.events.includes(event)` in memory. This avoided wrong dispatch but still read unrelated webhook URLs, secret fields, and event arrays for every event dispatch.

The dispatch-time `registration.isActive && registration.events.includes(event)` filter remains as defense in depth, so legacy or unexpected rows still do not dispatch or persist delivery attempts for unsupported/nonmatching events. No schema, migration, frontend, auth/authz, retry behavior, external payload shape, URL validation, secret signing, or destructive behavior changed.
- performance issues found: reduced webhook dispatch registration reads from all active org registrations to active registrations subscribed to the requested event. This shrinks DB payload and Node filtering work on hot event dispatch paths.

### 20260625-0959 JST

- current task: implement Codex-owned `F-20260625-staff-workload-recent-tasks-batch` after Claude `LOCK_GRANT`, covering `/api/staff-workload` route-level performance without frontend, schema, migration, service, or auth changes.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox/history for Claude's `F-20260625-claude-patient-status-tracker-perf` completion, `F-20260625-staff-workload-recent-tasks-batch` lock grant, and long-gate release; `git status --short --branch --untracked-files=all`; `src/app/api/staff-workload/route.ts`; `src/app/api/staff-workload/route.test.ts`; `prisma/schema/medication.prisma` `Task` model and enum/index definitions; `prisma/schema/prescription.prisma` related assigned-task indexes; existing raw SQL/window-query patterns in `src/server/services/patient-service.ts`, `src/app/api/visit-records/route.ts`, and `src/server/services/workflow-dashboard-queries.ts`; read-only performance reviewer output for staff workload N+1 risk; independent DB reviewer output for raw SQL null-ordering semantics; gbrain `code_callers` and `code_blast` output for `src/app/api/staff-workload/route.ts::GET`.
- files changed: `src/app/api/staff-workload/route.ts`, `src/app/api/staff-workload/route.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `/api/staff-workload` fetched recent open tasks with `mapWithConcurrency(staffIds, 4, prisma.task.findMany(... take: 4))`, issuing one query per active staff member. The test suite also encoded the staff-count query pattern by expecting two `task.findMany` calls for two staff.
- security risks found: no auth/authz, tenant, PHI projection, response shape, DB schema, migration, billing, deploy, or destructive behavior changed. The new raw query preserves `org_id = ctx.orgId`, restricts `assigned_to` to the active staff id set, and keeps open-task statuses limited to `pending` / `in_progress`. It uses Prisma tagged `$queryRaw`, not unsafe string interpolation.
- performance issues found: reduced recent-open-task DB round trips from active-staff count to one batched query. The route now uses `ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY assigned_to ORDER BY sla_due_at ASC NULLS LAST, due_date ASC NULLS LAST, priority ASC, created_at DESC)` and `rn <= 4` to keep the same per-staff maximum while preserving open task counts, visit lookup, dispense task grouping, date filtering, and workload scoring. An intermediate `NULLS FIRST` draft was rejected by Claude review because it would rank undated tasks ahead of dated SLA tasks and change the capped top-four semantics; the final query pins `NULLS LAST`.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/staff-workload/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; focused `pnpm exec eslint src/app/api/staff-workload/route.ts src/app/api/staff-workload/route.test.ts`; focused `pnpm exec prettier --check src/app/api/staff-workload/route.ts src/app/api/staff-workload/route.test.ts`; focused `git diff --check` for the two staff-workload files; long-gate-locked `pnpm typecheck`.
- validation results: focused staff-workload Vitest passed `3/3`; focused ESLint passed; focused Prettier passed; focused diff-check passed; `pnpm typecheck` passed under local long-gate lock after `next typegen` and both TypeScript projects completed successfully. After peer review, nullable ordering was corrected to explicit `NULLS LAST` and the focused validation bundle was rerun. Tests now assert `task.findMany` is not called, `$queryRaw` is called once with the org id and active staff ids, the SQL includes `ROW_NUMBER()`, `PARTITION BY assigned_to`, `sla_due_at ASC NULLS LAST`, `due_date ASC NULLS LAST`, `org_id =`, `assigned_to = ANY(...)`, `status IN ('pending', 'in_progress')`, and `WHERE rn <=`, and the response still caps each staff member's rendered open tasks at four.
- remaining work: send `READY_FOR_REVIEW` to Claude with validation evidence, wait for approval, then stage and commit only the two staff-workload files plus `.codex/ralph-state.md`. Migration/index follow-ups remain separate and human-gated if schema changes are proposed.
- next action: drain agmsg, run final formatting/diff checks including this ledger entry, send `READY_FOR_REVIEW`, handle Claude feedback, and commit after approval.

The patch deliberately does not touch `src/server/jobs/runner.ts`, persisted job `error_log`, `/admin/jobs`, job success payload `errors`, auth/authz, tenant scope, DB schema, migrations, external calls, billing, deploy, or destructive behavior. Broader `/admin/jobs` persisted error-log redaction remains a human-gated `BLOCKED.md` item.
- performance issues found: no runtime performance path changed. The patch adds one safe logger call per failure path and only affects error logging; fetch/job execution counts, authentication, side effects, and response payloads are preserved. Read-only follow-up candidate found: `/api/staff-workload` recent-task lookup has staff-count N+1 queries and can likely be batched with a route-only PostgreSQL window query, with migration/index work deferred separately.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/jobs/flush-metrics/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/jobs/[jobType]/route.test.ts' src/app/api/admin/flush-metrics/route.test.ts src/lib/utils/logger.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; focused `pnpm exec eslint` for the six API route/test files; focused `pnpm exec prettier --check` for the six API route/test files; focused `git diff --check` for the six API route/test files; `rg` scan confirming only `logger.error` remains under the touched job/flush routes; long-gate-locked `pnpm typecheck`.

- bugs found: the schedules day board mixed all-time workload counters into a date-scoped UI: completed dispense audit tasks were counted across the org instead of the current day's schedule cycles, and report-pending count used an all-time `medicationCycle.count` for `visit_completed` cycles. The page also issued a separate `/api/tasks?status=open&task_types=...` request, then filtered org-wide task results client-side to the visible board.
- security risks found: F-022 reduces schedule-board task overexposure by moving operational task retrieval into the day-board BFF, applying the existing dashboard assignment scope, filtering to current visible visit/proposal entity ids, selecting only bounded presentation fields, and serializing `metadata: null`. Tests assert task metadata and memo-like values are not exposed through the schedule board response. No auth, RLS, DB schema, migration, API mutation semantics, audit log, secret, billing/payment, deploy, or destructive operation changed.

Raw holiday ids remain preserved for React Query keys, editing/delete state, request bodies, POST collection path semantics, invalidation keys, and API route identity. No auth, RLS, DB schema, migration, API body contract, PHI projection, logging, secret, billing/payment, deploy, or destructive operation changed.
- performance issues found: no runtime performance path changed. The patch adds small shared-helper calls and hoists one bulk JSON header object outside the per-date loop while preserving fetch count per selected date, query keys, invalidation fan-out, payload shape, and rendered UI structure.

- performance issues found: no query, mutation, cache, network, or rendering algorithm changed. The save blocker checks whether any already-rendered row has a trimmed name.

### 20260620-0153 JST

- current task: harden patient-share-consent revoke so the high-risk consent撤回 action requires a reason and a `ConfirmDialog` before calling the revoke API.
- files inspected: `docs/ui-ux-design-guidelines.md`, `src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.test.tsx`, accessibility reviewer findings, validation output, and current git status.
- files changed: `src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.test.tsx`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: patient-share-consent revoke accepted a reason input but the revoke button was only disabled by busy state, used a non-destructive variant, and called the revoke mutation directly. That allowed accidental or reasonless consent撤回 attempts without the shared confirmation gate used by other pharmacy-cooperation state transitions. The initial concurrent change also used the current selected share-case ID in the revoke URL; this was fixed to use the consent's own `share_case_id` captured in the pending action.
- security risks found: reduced consent/PHI-sharing governance risk by requiring a trimmed revoke reason, forcing a destructive confirmation dialog with consent ID/date/share-case/pharmacy context, making the mutation reject empty reasons before sending any request body, and binding the request to the consent's share-case ID. No auth, permission, DB schema, PHI projection, message content, or unrelated workflow API behavior changed.
- performance issues found: no query, polling, invalidation, or render performance issue was found or changed. The update is local state-gating and one extra confirmation step before the existing revoke mutation.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write 'src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.test.tsx' tools/tests/ui-route-mocked-smoke.spec.ts`; `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.test.tsx' tools/tests/ui-route-mocked-smoke.spec.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.test.tsx' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm typecheck`; `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm format:check`; `git diff --check`; focused `rg` scan for revoke wiring.
- validation results: targeted Prettier passed unchanged. Targeted ESLint passed. Initial focused Vitest failed because the confirmation detail shortened the consent ID; the detail was changed to show the full consent ID, then focused Vitest passed with 1 file / 12 tests. After adding share-case ID binding, focused Vitest passed again with 1 file / 12 tests. `pnpm typecheck` passed. `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm format:check` passed. `git diff --check` passed. The focused scan confirmed the revoke mutation is now only invoked through the pending workflow action path and still rejects empty reasons in the mutation function.
- remaining work: pharmacy-cooperation UI/UX remediation remains active. Browser/a11y proof was expanded in the next slice. Remaining high/medium candidates include patient-link acceptance context and repeated workflow/admin row action names.
- next action: commit workflow consent-revoke implementation/test, commit browser proof test, then commit ledger separately.

- bugs found: v0.2 share scopes already contained `attachments`, `print`, `pdf_output`, and `download`, but the R2 rule mapping output actions to required scope keys was not centralized. That left future attachment/PDF/print/download routes at risk of interpreting the same share scope differently.

No API, DB schema, PHI projection, notification, or route behavior was changed in this slice.

The response is now no-store and minimized to safe status/link/partnership fields with `has_partner_patient_id` instead of raw snapshots.

### 20260620-0820 JST

- current task: D2 backend-perf-bulkify delegation from Claude, replacing selected-task completion fan-out with one scoped bulk task update.
- files inspected: `src/app/(dashboard)/tasks/tasks-content.tsx`, `src/app/api/tasks/route.ts`, `src/app/api/tasks/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/tasks/[id]/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/tasks/route.test.ts`, `src/lib/tasks/inline-completion.ts`, `src/lib/validations/task.ts`, `src/server/services/dashboard-assignment-scope.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-write-guard.ts`, `src/server/services/workflow-dashboard-queries.ts`, and current git status/diff.
- files changed: `src/app/(dashboard)/tasks/tasks-content.tsx`, `src/app/api/tasks/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/tasks/bulk/route.ts`, `src/app/api/tasks/bulk/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/task-write-guard.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: selected task completion sent one `PATCH /api/tasks/:id` per selected row, multiplying HTTP requests and duplicating conflict handling on the client. The write guard for PHI-backed task completion was also local to the single-task route, which would have made bulk completion easy to drift from the existing patient/case archive checks.

### 20260620-1245 JST

- current task: fix the High re-audit finding where print hub could reuse cached visit-report preview body before the current `preview_rendered` audit completed.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox output, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/ui-ux-design-guidelines.md`, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/05-server-and-client-components.md`, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/06-fetching-data.md`, `src/app/(dashboard)/reports/[id]/print/page.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/reports/[id]/print/page.test.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.test.tsx`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state file.
- files changed: `src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.test.tsx`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: print hub's visit-report print-audit query key was scoped only by org/report id, so React Query could expose cached audited body text while a new `preview_rendered` POST was pending or failing. The print button disabled state also only checked pending/error, not whether the current audit response had `audited === true` and a report payload.
- security risks found: PHI/medical report preview could be displayed from stale cached audit data before the current preview audit succeeded. The fix gates report body rendering and printing on the current audit run and keeps the existing fail-closed API behavior.
- performance issues found: no material runtime cost. The query key gains a per-mount nonce and the same single preview audit POST still runs for visit-report previews.

### 20260622-1931 JST

- current task: codex-owned F-20260622-006 patient MCS query URL path-segment hardening.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox output, `src/lib/patient-mcs/query.ts`, `src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts`, related patient MCS UI route usages found by code-mapper, `.agent-loop/REVIEW_LOG.md`, `.agent-loop/VERIFY_LOG.md`, and this Ralph state file.
- files changed: `src/lib/patient-mcs/query.ts`, `src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts`, `.agent-loop/REVIEW_LOG.md`, `.agent-loop/VERIFY_LOG.md`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `fetchPatientMcsOverview` interpolated raw `patientId` into `/api/patients/${patientId}/mcs`, so a hostile or malformed id containing `/`, `?`, or `#` could change the requested route/query/fragment. Fixed by encoding only the path segment with `encodeURIComponent(patientId)` while preserving raw query keys for cache identity.
- security risks found: reduced a defense-in-depth path traversal / route confusion risk in the patient MCS fetch helper. No auth, billing, payments, RLS, DB, migration, production deploy, secret, cookie, PHI logging, or destructive behavior was touched. Claude confirmed this is not a §15 hard-stop because server-side authz/normalization remains the primary boundary.
- performance issues found: no material runtime performance change; `encodeURIComponent` is constant-time relative to the existing single fetch path and does not alter cache keys or request fan-out.
- validation commands: baseline `pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; focused final Vitest for the same file; scoped `pnpm exec eslint --max-warnings=0 src/lib/patient-mcs/query.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts`; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --check src/lib/patient-mcs/query.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts`; scoped `git diff --check -- src/lib/patient-mcs/query.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts`; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm build`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`.
- validation results: baseline Vitest 6/6 passed; final focused Vitest 7/7 passed; scoped ESLint, Prettier, and diff-check passed; `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm build` passed after removing stale `.next/dev/lock` with no live Next build holder; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused` passed serially after build. Claude PATCH_REVIEW_RESULT approved rev1.
- remaining work: stage only the two patient-mcs files plus F-006 ledger rows, commit, send DONE/release for F-006 source and ledger locks, then return to autonomous next-candidate intake. UI direct MCS path interpolations are intentionally deferred to Claude-owned follow-up (`F-20260622-007` candidate) because they live under `src/app/(dashboard)/**` / `src/components/**`.

### 20260624-0646 JST

- current task: codex-owned F-20260624-002 patient timeline backend hardening.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox/plan-review/patch-review messages for F-20260624-002, `src/server/services/patient-detail.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail.test.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-tasks.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-tasks.test.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-timeline-events.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-timeline-query.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-timeline-query.test.ts`, `src/app/api/patients/[id]/timeline/route.ts`, `src/app/api/patients/[id]/detail-slices.test.ts`, `prisma/schema/admin.prisma`, `prisma/schema/medication.prisma`, `tools/scripts/prepare-e2e-db-core.ts`, `package.json`, and this Ralph state file.
- files changed: `src/server/services/patient-detail.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail.test.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-tasks.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-tasks.test.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-timeline-events.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-timeline-query.test.ts`, `src/app/api/patients/[id]/detail-slices.test.ts`, `prisma/schema/admin.prisma`, `prisma/schema/medication.prisma`, `prisma/migrations/20260624063000_patient_timeline_backend_hardening/migration.sql`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `getPatientTimelineData` had an unbounded first-visit document timeline query, fail-fast timeline source fan-out that could turn one source outage into a full timeline 500, server-timezone-sensitive date labels that could show the wrong JST day/month, and nondeterministic ordering for same-timestamp cross-source timeline events. The AuditLog operation-history query also lacked a target-scoped composite index, but adding `patient_id` to the predicate would have dropped legacy/export audit rows where `patient_id` is null.
- security risks found: reduced audit/timeline performance and availability risk by bounding first-visit documents, adding additive target/date indexes, making only the timeline source batch fail-soft, and keeping legacy target-scoped AuditLog filters without a lossy `patient_id` predicate. Partial failure logging is redacted to `{ orgId, source, error: error.name }`, and the client-facing `partial_failures` message is generic. Did not wrap the long fan-out in one `withOrgContext` interactive transaction because that would reintroduce timeout risk and defeat fail-soft behavior; explicit `org_id` filters remain the enforced isolation for this pass. Prod migration apply remains human-gated.
- performance issues found: added `@@index([org_id, target_type, target_id, created_at(sort: Desc)])` for AuditLog timeline operation history and `@@index([org_id, patient_id, created_at(sort: Desc)])` for FirstVisitDocument timeline reads; added `take: 8` to first-visit documents; raised timeline-only source concurrency to 8; overlapped source actor-name resolution with the AuditLog query. No cursor pagination or source-adapter registry was bundled.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/api/patients/[id]/detail-slices.test.ts' src/server/services/patient-detail-tasks.test.ts src/server/services/patient-detail-timeline-query.test.ts src/server/services/patient-detail.test.ts`; scoped `pnpm exec eslint` on changed TS/TSX files; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --check` on changed TS/TSX files after formatting; scoped `git diff --check`; `pnpm exec prisma validate`; local e2e `DATABASE_URL=...localhost:5433... pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma migrate deploy --schema=prisma/schema/`; local e2e `prisma migrate status`; node `pg` query against local e2e `pg_indexes`; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; Claude high-risk CODE_REVIEW.
- validation results: focused Vitest passed 4 files / 106 tests; scoped ESLint passed; scoped Prettier passed; scoped diff-check passed; Prisma schema validate passed; local e2e migration deploy applied `20260624063000_patient_timeline_backend_hardening`; migrate status reported up to date; node `pg` confirmed `AuditLog_org_target_created_idx` and `FirstVisitDocument_org_patient_created_idx`; `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused` passed. Claude CODE_REVIEW_RESULT approved rev1 after independently re-running the focused 106-test bundle and noted only a non-blocking prod-deploy advisory to consider `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` or a maintenance window for the human-gated production apply. Source commit `a0dfa217` contains only the F-20260624-002 owned source/schema/test/migration files.

### 20260625-1027 JST

- current task: codex-owned backend hardening pivot for F-20260625-prescription-intakes-qr-null-mismatch, after closing F-20260625-today-preparation-audit-queue-take-one as a false-positive with Claude.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox/history for Claude grants and ACKs, `.agent-loop/research/workflow-findings-20260625.md`, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md`, `src/app/api/visits/today-preparation/route.ts`, `src/app/api/visits/today-preparation/route.test.ts`, `prisma/schema/prescription.prisma`, `src/app/api/admin/organizations/route.ts`, `src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts`, `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts`, `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`, gbrain refs/call graph outputs, and read-only subagent findings.
- files changed: `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts`, `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: the QR draft line mismatch guard compared a submitted line only when the draft side had a non-null value. If the draft omitted an optional field such as `drugCode` but the submitted request supplied `drug_code`, the mismatch could be skipped and the QR draft could be claimed into an intake despite the request/draft line not matching.
- security risks found: reduced medication-data integrity and patient-safety risk by making QR draft line confirmation compare the normalized request and draft values bidirectionally. Empty-vs-empty still matches, but either side having a value while the other side is absent now blocks confirmation before `qrScanDraft.updateMany`, intake creation, realtime broadcast, and webhook side effects. No auth/RLS/schema/service/UI, production deploy, secret, cookie, PHI logging, or destructive behavior changed.
- performance issues found: no meaningful runtime performance change. The route still compares the same bounded per-line fields and only removes the one-sided skip that caused false negatives. Separately, the proposed `today-preparation` `dispenseTask.findMany take:10 -> take:1` perf slice was rejected as unsafe because the route post-filters latest audit result and then re-sorts narcotic tasks before returning `next_action`.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped `pnpm exec eslint --max-warnings=0 src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --check .codex/ralph-state.md src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`; scoped `git diff --check -- .codex/ralph-state.md src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck`.
- validation results: focused prescription-intakes route Vitest passed 1 file / 32 tests after adding no-side-effect assertions for both QR line mismatch tests; scoped ESLint passed; scoped Prettier passed; scoped diff-check passed; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` passed after the final assertion update. Claude granted the route-only lock and confirmed the today-preparation auditQueue item as a false-positive. Code-mapper subagent independently prioritized the QR mismatch route-only bug; data-integrity subagent independently confirmed the today-preparation `take:1` change would be behavior-breaking. Privacy review found one low test-coverage gap for `notifyWorkflowMutation` side effects, which was closed; medical-safety review found no issues and confirmed the stricter draft/request comparison is safety-positive. Claude CODE_REVIEW_RESULT approved rev2 and independently ran 66 route tests/teeth, with only a non-blocking follow-up suggestion for a positive both-sides-absent `drug_code` happy path.
- remaining work: drain agmsg, run final scoped ledger checks, stage only the two prescription-intakes files plus this Ralph entry, commit the approved slice, and notify Claude.
- next action: commit the approved route-only slice, keep untracked plan artifacts out of staging, then request/lock the handed-off `handoff-board/items` `is_active` route fix as the next Codex route slice.

### 20260625-1159 JST

- current task: codex-owned F-20260625-care-reports-case-org-recheck route hardening, after committing F-20260625-prescription-intakes-qr-null-mismatch as `dd30e0fa`.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox/history for Claude handoff and GRANT, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md`, `src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts`, `src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts`, gbrain call-graph/blast outputs, and read-only security reviewer output.
- files changed: `src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts`, `src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: after `validateCareReportSource` resolved a case id, the POST path reused that id for `careReport.create`. The later enrichment lookup checked only `id` and `org_id`, treated a missing row as optional, and still created the report. A stale/deleted/reassigned case between validation and create could therefore leave a report linked to a no-longer-valid case id or fail open to a DB/runtime error instead of returning a validation response.
- security risks found: reduced patient/case tenant-boundary defense-in-depth risk by rechecking the resolved case with `id`, `org_id`, and `patient_id` immediately before enrichment/create, and by returning the existing generic case/patient validation error before `withOrgContext` or `careReport.create` side effects. No PHI is added to logs or error payloads, and patient-only reports with no resolved case id remain allowed.
- performance issues found: no meaningful runtime performance change. The existing enrichment lookup is kept but made fail-closed and patient-scoped; no new fan-out, pagination, schema, service, or UI work was introduced.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped `pnpm exec eslint --max-warnings=0 src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts`; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --check .codex/ralph-state.md src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts` after formatting; scoped `git diff --check -- .codex/ralph-state.md src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck`.
- validation results: focused care-reports route Vitest passed 1 file / 40 tests; scoped ESLint passed; scoped Prettier passed after formatting the test file; scoped diff-check passed; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` passed. Security reviewer confirmed the fail-closed guard is appropriate and noted this current checkout now has the intended guard in place. Privacy reviewer found no PHI/PII exposure, tenant/access-scope expansion, validation-before-write regression, or response-shape regression.
- remaining work: send READY_FOR_REVIEW to Claude, then commit only the two care-reports files plus this Ralph entry if approved.
- next action: submit the slice to Claude reviewer-audit and wait for approval before commit.

### 20260625-1320 JST

- current task: codex-owned F-20260625-outbound-webhook-no-redirect SSRF hardening after Claude finished day-board handoff and granted the outbound-webhook service/test lock.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox/history for Claude GRANT, CODE_REVIEW_RESULT approval, and §15-adjacent constraints, `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.ts`, `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.test.ts`, gbrain definition/reference outputs for `dispatchToEndpoint`, security reviewer output, strict reviewer output, and current git status.
- files changed: `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.ts`, `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: outbound webhook dispatch validated the registered HTTPS URL before sending, but the actual `fetch` used default redirect behavior. A public, allowed webhook endpoint could return a 30x redirect to a private/link-local/internal URL after validation, creating a post-validation SSRF bypass.
- security risks found: reduced webhook redirect SSRF risk by setting `redirect: 'manual'` on the dispatch fetch. Redirect responses now remain ordinary non-OK delivery failures with the original registered URL and status code; the redirect `Location` is not followed, persisted, or returned. Security review found no blocker in this diff, but identified a remaining pre-existing DNS rebinding class where validation DNS and fetch connection DNS can differ; that needs a separate transport/resolver hardening slice.
- performance issues found: no meaningful runtime performance change. No new DB reads, loops, retries, or concurrency changes were introduced.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/server/services/outbound-webhook.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped `pnpm exec eslint --max-warnings=0 src/server/services/outbound-webhook.ts src/server/services/outbound-webhook.test.ts`; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --check src/server/services/outbound-webhook.ts src/server/services/outbound-webhook.test.ts .codex/ralph-state.md`; scoped `git diff --check -- src/server/services/outbound-webhook.ts src/server/services/outbound-webhook.test.ts .codex/ralph-state.md`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck`; read-only security reviewer; strict reviewer.
- validation results: focused outbound-webhook Vitest passed 1 file / 20 tests after strengthening the redirect test with single-fetch and no-link-local-follow assertions; scoped ESLint passed; scoped Prettier passed; scoped diff-check passed; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` passed before the test/ledger-only strengthening. Security reviewer confirmed the no-redirect diff blocks the 30x SSRF bypass without over-blocking normal 2xx webhooks or leaking redirect locations. Strict reviewer found no source blocker after the test/ledger wording was tightened. Claude CODE_REVIEW_RESULT approved the slice for commit.
- remaining work: commit only the outbound-webhook source/test plus this Ralph entry, then notify Claude. Track DNS rebinding SSRF as a separate slice rather than silently treating this redirect fix as full SSRF closure. Follow-up candidates remain dependency audit upgrade, visit-billing candidate N+1 batching, and daily billing-evidence bounded anti-join; Data Explorer high-risk write hardening and set-photo binding are §15-adjacent and should be escalated rather than auto-landed.
- next action: commit the approved F-20260625-outbound-webhook-no-redirect slice, notify Claude, then continue with the dependency audit slice under Claude's granted package lock and full-gate conditions.

- bugs found: the business-holidays GET list accepted unvalidated `date_from`, `date_to`, and `site_id` query parameters and sent the list query without an explicit bound. Blank `site_id` silently behaved like no filter, invalid dates flowed to `new Date(...)`, reversed date ranges were not rejected before DB access, and very large list requests were unconstrained.
- security risks found: reduced external-input and tenant-scoped list-hardening risk by validating date filters with the existing date-key semantics, rejecting reversed ranges and blank `site_id` before Prisma, preserving `canAdmin` and `org_id: ctx.orgId`, and adding tests that lock the response envelope and permission options. No POST/create/audit behavior, schema, auth/RLS, PHI logging, production deploy, or destructive operation was changed.
- performance issues found: bounded the admin holiday list query with `take`, defaulting to 100 and clamping at 400. This prevents accidental full-table scans for the org holiday configuration list while preserving existing filter/include/order response shape.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/business-holidays/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped `pnpm exec eslint src/app/api/business-holidays/route.ts src/app/api/business-holidays/route.test.ts`; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --check src/app/api/business-holidays/route.ts src/app/api/business-holidays/route.test.ts` after formatting; scoped `git diff --check -- src/app/api/business-holidays/route.ts src/app/api/business-holidays/route.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck`; read-only API contract subagent review.

API contract reviewer found no breaking contract issue and raised one low test-hardening gap, which was closed by asserting the GET response envelope and captured `canAdmin` options. - performance issues found: blank-present filters now fail before patient/report/delivery summary queries, avoiding accidental broad report list and delivery-summary reads caused by empty filter values. No new DB reads or N+1 behavior were introduced.

- files changed: `src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/route-catalog.ts`, `src/lib/api/route-catalog.test.ts`, `src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `GET /api/dashboard/medication-deadlines` accepted padded `within_days`, blank/padded `limit`, blank/padded `q`, duplicate known filters, and overlong search strings as normalized or first-value inputs, allowing malformed medication-deadline requests to proceed into PHI-bearing visit-schedule reads. The route also returned patient IDs/names, case IDs, visit schedule dates, medication end dates, and pharmacist IDs without sensitive no-store HTTP headers, and was missing route catalog coverage.
- security risks found: reduced PHI/medication-deadline caching and caller-intent drift risk by rejecting malformed `within_days`, `limit`, and `q` before `prisma.visitSchedule.findMany`, wrapping the authenticated GET handler with `withSensitiveNoStore`, and registering `/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines` in route catalog plus protected GET no-store assertions. Validation errors use fixed messages and field names only; raw query values are not echoed. Auth, org scoping, valid global-search caller behavior, response envelope, schema, migrations, external sends, and destructive operations were not changed.
- performance issues found: malformed medication-deadline filters now fail before the visit-schedule read and patient-name relation filter, avoiding accidental broad deadline reads and unnecessary query work. No new list reads, heavier projections, or N+1 paths were introduced.
- validation commands: `pnpm prettier --write src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.ts src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.test.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`; focused `pnpm vitest run src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.test.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; focused `pnpm eslint src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.ts src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.test.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`; focused `pnpm prettier --check ...`; focused `git diff --check -- ...`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` under token `B7155586-7E79-4304-84CD-02AB32F75A9A`; read-only privacy compliance reviewer; read-only API contract reviewer.
- validation results: focused dashboard medication-deadlines/protected GET/catalog Vitest passed `4` files / `188` tests after adding default-7-days compatibility, valid `q`/`limit` compatibility, malformed/duplicate query rejection before visit-schedule reads, no-store assertions, route catalog, and protected GET coverage. Focused ESLint passed; focused Prettier check passed; focused diff whitespace check passed; `pnpm typecheck` passed and the local long-gate lock was released. Privacy reviewer returned PASS with no blockers. API contract reviewer returned PASS with no blockers.
